finding.next_action: Exhaustively inventory all active SIDs/BIDs via county clerk ordinance lists, Appendix 28-A updates, recent bills, and finance/tax assessment records for Honolulu (confirm only Nos. 1-3), Hawaiʻi (confirm only Kailua Village No. 1; Hilo rejected), Maui (search for any established under Ch. 3.70), and Kauai (confirm none); extract or request parcel-level boundaries/maps/GIS from district associations, county real property tax offices, or open data portals (none found statewide or countywide); recheck DCCA business registry only for management nonprofits and DBEDT/AG for any overlooked registry.
finding.notes: Statewide enabling is HRS §46-80.5 (Special improvement district), allowing charter counties to create SIDs by ordinance for supplemental maintenance/security, environmental/natural resource/hazard/climate work, and other services/improvements that restore/promote business activity, via special assessments (flexible methodology), optional bonds, and nonprofit association management (Ch. 414D). These are the legal BID equivalents. Honolulu implements via ROH Ch. 28 (SIDs Nos. 1 Waikiki ~2000, 2 Fort Street Mall expanded 2025 via Bill 51/Ord. 25-47 to Downtown Honolulu BID, 3 Waikiki Beach 2015); Hawaiʻi County via Code Ch. 35 (explicitly BIDs; active Kailua Village No. 1; Downtown Hilo No. 2 proposed but rejected 2023); Maui via Ord. 4382 adding Ch. 3.70 (enabling only; no active districts confirmed); Kauai has not authorized per community plan. Distinguish from traditional improvement districts (HRS 46-80), community facilities districts (HRS 46-80.1 / ROH Ch. 27), tax increment, zoning/design special districts (e.g., Honolulu zoning special districts GIS—different purpose/use), and HCDA districts. No statewide registry, comprehensive official list, or unified GIS of BID/SID boundaries (statewide geoportal and county open data lack them; district sites provide some maps/descriptions). Coverage partial: authority + all county enabling codes + known active districts (Honolulu-centric + one Hawaiʻi) verified with high confidence; full active inventory, status of any Maui districts, and digital boundaries incomplete. Sparse/failed searches (e.g., Kauai active, statewide maps) do not prove absence.
